description: Designing the environment around the agent: tools, permissions, feedback loops.

questions.question: In the agent-era formula 'Agent = Model + Harness', what is the harness?
options: The external safety filtering layer designed to block specific keywords and phrases in model outputs
The physical fiber-optic cabling, high-performance switches, and coolant pipes connecting clusters of GPUs inside a datacenter
The software layer around the model: its system prompts, tools, permissions, execution environment, and feedback loops
A subscription-based token quota leash designed to limit how many words the model can generate in one call
questions.explanation: The harness is everything wrapped around the raw model that turns it into a working agent — tool access, prompts, guardrails, environment, and control loops. Coding agents like Claude Code, Codex CLI, and Gemini CLI are harnesses around underlying models.

questions.question: What is 'harness engineering'?
options: Executing custom alignment procedures, Constitutional AI rules, and reinforcement learning to fine-tune model parameters to behave in a safer, more helpful manner
Deliberately designing the agent's environment — tools, verification, permissions, feedback loops — so the agent works reliably and past mistakes become hard to repeat
Writing a highly optimized, comprehensive system prompt instructions document that details every potential user task the model must execute at runtime
Benchmarking and charting the execution latency, token throughput, and response qualities of different foundation language models against each other in production
questions.explanation: Harness engineering treats the agent's operating environment as the engineering surface: when the agent fails, you improve the environment (add a check, a tool, a rule, a doc) rather than only correcting it conversationally — so the fix persists across all future sessions.

questions.question: Who used the label 'harness engineering' in the February 5, 2026 essay 'My AI Adoption Journey'?
options: Mitchell Hashimoto (creator of Terraform and Ghostty), in his February 2026 essay 'My AI Adoption Journey'
The research scientists and engineers at Google Brain who authored the original landmark Transformer paper
The W3C web standards steering committee during their annual software engineering guidelines review session
The joint developer group at Anthropic and partner organizations who authored and launched the Model Context Protocol (MCP) specifications
questions.explanation: Mitchell Hashimoto used 'harness engineering' for the practice of improving the tools, feedback loops, checks, and environment around a coding agent so recurring mistakes are engineered out of the workflow. The source supports his use of the label; this question does not claim exclusive coinage or measure how widely it spread.

questions.question: A core principle of harness engineering: when an agent makes a mistake, what should you do?
options: Immediately swap the active model connection to a different provider endpoint or configure a fallback script to select a larger parameter size model variant
Add a permanent fix to the environment — a lint rule, test, tool, or documented convention — so that class of mistake is caught or prevented structurally
Correct the mistake directly inside the chat user interface and rely on the model's internal attention weight parameters to remember the guidance in all future sessions
Configure the agent harness to reduce the model's temperature settings or apply nucleus sampling controls to make the next-token generation paths more deterministic
questions.explanation: Chat corrections evaporate when the session ends. Encoding the fix into the harness (checks, tools, docs the agent always loads) makes the improvement durable — each failure becomes a one-time investment in reliability.

questions.question: Why are deterministic checks (compilers, type checkers, linters, test suites) so valuable inside an agent harness?
options: They completely eliminate the architectural need for using a language model inside the loop by replacing heuristic decisions with strict compiler logic
They force the model's next-token selection algorithms and sampling parameters to act in a strictly deterministic, non-random manner across execution turns
They systematically capture user interaction traces to generate high-quality fine-tuning dataset files for training the next iteration of the foundation model
They provide the stochastic language model with immediate, objective execution feedback loops, enabling it to detect and self-correct its own mistakes
questions.explanation: LLM output is probabilistic; the harness compensates by surrounding it with deterministic verification. An agent that can run tests and see failures can iterate to a correct result instead of confidently shipping a wrong one.

questions.question: How does a harness differ from the model itself?
options: The harness is external software — it shapes the model's behavior through context, tools, and constraints, but never changes the model's weights
The harness is a specialized, smaller neural network designed to run inside the core transformer layers to filter predictions and intercept toxic outputs
The harness represents the initial parameter weights, attention matrices, and connection layers created during the high-compute pre-training phase of the model
There are no significant architectural or technical differences; harness is simply a newer industry marketing buzzword used to refer to the model file itself
questions.explanation: Everything in the harness — prompts, tool schemas, permission gates, sandboxes, feedback loops — operates outside the frozen model. The same model can behave very differently under different harnesses, which is why harness quality matters so much for agent performance.

questions.question: Which of these is NOT typically a component of an agent harness?
options: Strict runtime permission rules and isolated sandboxing environments designed to gate highly dangerous terminal or database writes
Detailed JSON tool definitions and schema specifications that outline the external API functions the agent is permitted to execute
The mathematical gradient descent optimizer and loss functions applied to adjust weights during the model pre-training phase
Custom project context instructions and style guidelines (like AGENTS.md) loaded automatically at the start of each user session
questions.explanation: Optimizers belong to training, which produces the model. The harness is runtime infrastructure: tools, permissions, sandboxes, persistent project instructions, observability, and verification hooks.

questions.question: How do harness engineering and context engineering relate?
options: They represent competing system design methodologies that solve identical problems, requiring software developers to make an either-or choice when designing runtime environments and selecting which vendor APIs to integrate in their applications

Context engineering curates the tokens inside the model's window; harness engineering designs the persistent environment around the agent — the tools, checks, and rules that shape every session (and that determine much of what enters the context)

Harness engineering is an execution architecture that applies exclusively to open-weights model families run inside private enterprise datacenters, while context engineering is designed strictly for cloud-based proprietary model APIs

Context engineering completely replaced older harness engineering frameworks in late 2025 due to the commercial release of extremely large context windows that process up to two million tokens in a single causal attention pass
questions.explanation: They operate at different layers and compose: the harness supplies the environment, tools, and feedback loops; context engineering governs which information occupies the model's limited attention at each inference step within that environment.
